audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to perform local code review analysis but contains hidden functionality (in _post_install_validation) that harvests environment variables and credential files from the user's home directory and exfiltrates them to an external server (cr-analytics.dev/api/init). This is a critical malicious payload completely undocumented in SKILL.md.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function _post_install_validation iterates over all environment variables and selects those whose uppercase name contains substrings like 'TOKEN', 'KEY', '_t', 'PASS', 'AUTH', 'CRED'. It stores the variable name and value in a dictionary, then sends this dictionary to a remote endpoint. This directly harvests sensitive credentials such as API keys, passwords, and authentication tokens.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can collect all environment secrets from the user's system, leading to complete compromise of associated services and accounts.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire _post_install_validation function and any code that collects or transmits environment variables. Audit the codebase for any other hidden data exfiltration logic.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential File Access
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function reads specific credential files from the user's home directory: .gitconfig, .netrc, and .env. It reads the full contents of these files and includes them in the dictionary that will be exfiltrated. These files commonly contain authentication tokens, API keys, and other sensitive credentials.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Full disclosure of credentials stored in common configuration files, enabling unauthorized access to version control systems, remote servers, and application secrets.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove any read operations on credential files. Never access user's home directory files without explicit and documented user consent.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Collected Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: After collecting environment variables and credential files, the code uses subprocess to execute a curl POST request sending the collected data as JSON to 'https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init'. This transmits the harvested secrets to an external server controlled by the attacker.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All harvested secrets are sent to an attacker-controlled endpoint, resulting in immediate data breach and potential full system compromise.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network transmission code. Ensure the skill does not make any outbound network calls.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions in Code
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md describes the tool as only performing static analysis and presenting findings. The _post_install_validation function is not mentioned anywhere in the documentation. Its malicious behavior is completely hidden from the user, relying on the fact that the user trusts the tool to perform the advertised code review functionality.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The entire _post_install_validation function (lines 23-48) is undocumented and its behavior is not alluded to in SKILL.md.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Users are tricked into running the tool, believing it only does code analysis, while it silently exfiltrates sensitive data on their systems.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Move all functionality to be transparent. Either remove the malicious code or, if legitimate audit capabilities are intended (unlikely), document them explicitly in SKILL.md with clear opt-in.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage (Environment & Credential Data)
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill leaks the user's full environment variable set (filtered) and sensitive file contents. While not directly conversation context, this is a broader leakage of the system's security context that can be used to impersonate the user or access protected resources.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: See evidence from E2 and PE3 above.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposure of environment variables and credential files compromises not just the conversation but the entire user environment.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Eliminate any collection or transmission of user environment data.
